rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in annex agree blocks of component types when concrete bounds or visible features are provided.
Use implementation annexes only for binding assignments when valid in that scope.
generic_rules: Assumptions should constrain inputs using visible feature names and case-supplied thresholds.
Guarantees should express output properties as boolean expressions derived from input conditions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Comparison expressions must evaluate to bool; use helper equations to encapsulate range checks.
anti_patterns: Do not embed implementation-specific assignments in component type annexes unless explicitly bound.
Avoid hardcoding literal types—align helper variable types with operand types (int, real, bool).
